description_long: This helicopter features a spherical fuselage with a segmented, partially transparent midsection, giving it a futuristic and modular appearance. The main rotor consists of several long, flat blades radiating from the top, while the tail section is compact and rounded, lacking a traditional tail boom. The exterior is a mix of dark and light materials, with the front section appearing more opaque and the midsection more translucent. There are protruding elements resembling teapots or mechanical arms attached to the sides, adding to its unconventional design. The overall structure suggests a blend of metallic and composite materials, with a focus on both form and function.
description: A spherical, segmented helicopter with a transparent midsection, long main rotor blades, and unusual side attachments.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main rotor blades radiating outward and the rounded, opaque front section with side attachments visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the spherical midsection, the segmented transparent panels, and the compact tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the rounded tail, the main rotor from above, and the side attachments extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the symmetry of the spherical body and the arrangement of the main rotor and tail.
attribute_rotor_blades: Eight long, flat, black blades radiate from the top, appearing to be made of composite material with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; the tail section is rounded and does not display a traditional tail rotor.
attribute_landing_skids: Not visible; the helicopter appears to lack traditional landing skids or gear.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Not visible; the front section is opaque and does not show distinct cockpit windows.
